source: VS Partners / Financial Times Global Seattle Symposium — Global technology investment: hype, hope, reality
summary: Blockchain/crypto is a genuine structural trend that counteracts AI centralization — bullish on the cycle
summary_extended: Baker argues that blockchain represents a genuine and important structural trend, not mere hype, because it serves as a counterforce to the centralizing tendencies of AI and big tech platforms by enabling decentralization and user control over identity and data. He believes the world is at the early stages of a powerful new blockchain-driven cycle, drawing an implicit parallel to prior technology cycles. A key part of his thesis is that blockchain-native startups face a more level playing field because they compete against other startups rather than entrenched mega-cap incumbents, making the space structurally attractive for new entrants. Baker uses hedged but directionally bullish language ('a real trend,' 'very important,' 'early stages'), though he does not name specific investments or price targets, leaving the thesis at a thematic rather than actionable level.
detail: Baker argues blockchain enables decentralization and user control over identity, directly opposing AI's centralizing force. He believes we are at the early stages of a powerful new blockchain-driven cycle. He notes blockchain is particularly advantageous for startups because they compete with other startups rather than mega-cap incumbents.
confidence_evidence: Baker calls it 'a real trend' and 'very important' but uses hedged language ('I do think,' 'fascinating to watch'). Does not name specific investments.
quote_fragment: we're at the early stages of a powerful new blockchain driven cycle
